label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Today is September 9, 1909. The question is asking what the date would be 24 hours later, and they want the answer in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, so I need to figure out which one is correct. First, I know that 24 hours later means exactly one full day has passed. So normally, adding one day to a date would just increment the day by one, unless there's some kind of boundary like the end of the month or a leap year or something. But September has 30 days, right? So September 9 plus one day would be September 10. The year is still 1909. So the date should be 09/10/1909. Let me check the options. Option A is 09/10/1909, which seems correct. The other options are either different months or different years. For example, option F is 09/10/1892, which is way in the past. That doesn't make sense. So the answer should be A. But let me make sure there's no trick here. Is there any possibility that adding 24 hours could cross into a different
